name: Recipefork Recipe Mode Switch
description: Two-mode authoring switch for choosing between Simple Mode and Chef Mode before starting or editing a recipe. Designed for Recipefork's dual authoring workflow.

split.description: Two equal-width tabs rendered as rich tiles with title, icon, and supporting description.

visual_brief: A card-contained two-mode switch where each option behaves like a tab tile rather than a plain text tab. Simple Mode uses a gentle utility sparkle icon with copy about fast creation. Chef Mode uses a chef-hat icon plus an 'Advanced' badge and copy about measurements, hierarchy, and deep editing. Active mode feels like a selected product tile.

responsive.mobile: Tiles stack comfortably inside a two-column tab rail with enough vertical padding to keep the mode distinction obvious.
responsive.tablet: Equal-width mode tiles with generous description text.
responsive.desktop: Wide card surface with supporting mode detail visible at a glance before the authoring form begins.
